title: 2024 Trek Marlin 5 - Gen 2
description:
Stolen Trek Marlin 5 – very distinctive color and setup, not a stock build.

Frame color: Volt Blue → Miami Green fade (bright, high-visibility gradient that really stands out)
Rear pannier rack/bag attached
Hybrid clip-in pedals (clip-in + flat combo)
Handlebar-mounted phone holder
Center/top tube bag mounted just behind the stem
Front and rear lights installed
Bell + horn on handlebars
Side-view mirrors on BOTH sides (uncommon and highly noticeable)

This bike is extremely recognizable due to the bright blue-to-green fade and the number of accessories. It looks more like a commuter/touring setup than a typical mountain bike.

If you see anything matching this description, even partially, please reach out.

title: 2008 Marin Bikes Mount Vision
description:
Brief description: Full suspension bike, 26” wheels, white with distinctive Purple brake handles, purple disc brake callipers, purple brake discs and golden wheel hubs. Marin “Bear” logo on front of bike and bar end caps.

Components: 
Forks: 		Fox Float 120 RL
Rear shock:	Fox RP3 - Kashima coated
Handlebars:	BrandX
Seat Post: 	BrandX dropper post
Brakes: 	Hope Tech E4 Callipers (in purple) with Tech 2 brake levers (in purple)
Disc rotors:	Hope 180mm front, 160mm rear (in purple)
Wheel hub:	Hope Evo 2 (in gold)
Wheel rims: 	26” Mavic XC717 Disc
Tires:
		- front: Shwalbe Hans Dampf
		- rear: Specialized (?) 
Gears:		Shimano XT groupset:
		- rear derailleur and cogs: Shimano XT
		- Cranks: Shimano XT (silver)
		- front shifter (right hand handlebar): Shimano XT
		- RaceFace narrow-wide 1x 32t front cog (on crank arm)
Accessories: Black Crud Guard (mountain bike mud guard) on main down tube attached with black rubber bands, small bits of green/yellow tape in places. Green ‘Hope’ sticker on lower part of swing arm by rear gears. Black neoprene/velcro chain protector on lower part of swing arm - right hand side only.
The bike had significant upgrades from the stock purchase, notably the braking system was added as an upgrade, as were the wheels, dropper seat post, handlebar and front cog.

title: 1994 Specialized Crossroads
description:
I came outside this morning 12/7/24 to find my prized commuter had disappeared from my backyard. Near City Park and Mountain. Likely stolen in the night. It was a barn find that I rebuilt and added many non-stock parts parts to, and I haven't seen seen anything similar to it around town. Unfortunately I did not write down the serial number, but I will be filing reports with Bike Index and the city.

~1994 Red Specialized Crossroads in XL/Large with White Decals

UPANBIKE butterfly bars Wrapped in Red (Most Distinctive)
Black Avid Speed Dial Brake Levers
Koski V-Brake front/ Avid Single Digit Rear Calipers
No-name 3x8 Speed Shifters
Shimano Deore LX Rear Derailleur
Vintage Shimano Exage? Front Derailleur
Puroma Red Pedals
Black Easton Front Wheel - Specialized Sawtooth Tire
Dura-Ace Hub With Mavic Rim Rear Wheel - Goodyear Transit Speed Tire
Silver Sunrace 11-40t Cassette on the Rear Wheel (Big Gear on back wheel)
Bontrager Seat

Other Stuff:

Faded Brown REI Flash Pannier on left side
Jim Blackburn Bottle Cage and JBlackburn Rear Rack
Adjustable Bottle Cage on seat tube
RockBros Tubetop Bag and Frame Bag
Polar Colorado Water Bottle
Red Phone Mount
Blitzu Bike Light (Paint Heavily Faded)
OnGuard U Lock (Unlocked)

title: Supercycle BREEZE
description:
Color: Dark blue frame with a glossy finish. The paint shows minor scuffs and wear, particularly near the bottom bracket and chainstay, reflecting typical usage.

Frame Size & Design: The bike features a medium-sized frame with a slightly slanted top tube. The tube joints are visibly welded and reinforced, providing extra durability for off-road or urban riding.

Gears & Drivetrain: Equipped with a standard multi-speed gear setup. It has a triple chainring in the front and a 7-speed cassette in the rear, allowing for smooth shifting over a range of terrains. The derailleurs and shifters are operational but show minor signs of use.

Brakes: The bike uses traditional rim brakes, both front and rear, which are in good working order. The brake levers on the handlebars are black and slightly worn from use.

Handlebars: The handlebars are flat, straight, and black, with wear visible on the grips. The right grip end has a small cap missing, exposing the inner handlebar tube. The handlebar stem shows slight rust and wear, while the cables for the brakes and shifters are externally routed, curving smoothly toward the frame.

Tires & Wheels: The tires are thick, knobby, and designed for multi-surface riding, with a deep tread pattern ideal for gravel or rough urban surfaces. The tires are mounted on aluminum rims, which appear to be in good shape. Both wheels are secured with standard quick-release mechanisms.

Saddle: The saddle is a basic black vinyl model, showing slight wear but no visible damage. It's supported by a standard seat post with a quick-release mechanism for easy height adjustments.

Water Bottle & Holder: White water bottle that says 'Silvera for Seniors' is a sipper style 500 ml bottle and is on a metal bottle cage that is located in the vertical rod of the bike just above the front gear chain

Accessories:

Lock: A black coiled cable lock is attached around the frame, securing the bike when parked.
Green Bag: A small green bag is mounted on the handlebar stem, possibly used for small items or tools.
Reflectors: There are red rear reflectors and front-mounted white reflectors for visibility.
Bar Code: The bike features a barcode sticker on the frame near the handlebar with the number "70410 01847."

Distinctive Marks: The handlebar stem has visible rust and slight wear, while the grips show signs of extended use.

title: Lectric eBikes XP foldable
description:
White with tritone blue design and lettering. Has a black cargo rack on the back. Tires are somewhere between 20 and 24 inches. Built in battery.
Found over Christmas/winter break, December 2025. Was "abandoned" along centre Ave and was lying unlocked and unattended in the grass between the road and the bike trail on the west side of Centre. After looking around and waiting 45 minutes or so, I decided to move it against the fence and put a note on it. The note basically called for the attention of the owner to claim the bike or risk forfeiture. I walked by the bike daily for 5 days, making sure it was still visible from the road and trail and that my note with phone number were still attached. On day 5, I was so excited that it was still there and it was like a Christmas present from the universe!!
Although the students aren't all back from their vacations, im pretty sure that it is still abandoned or unclaimed as there is no CSU registration sticker on it so must have had a non-student owner.
Anyway, it is now day 7 and I have brought the bike home to register and officially adopt !! :) yay

title: 2023 Cervélo ASPERO – 5 GRX RX815 Di2
description:
• Premium grade gravel race carbon GRX Di2/disc brake bicycle that improves on original design
• Upgraded carbon fiber, and lay-up schedule for stiffer, faster design with aerodynamic improvements
• Use of D-shaped, tapered carbon fork steerer design, with included bar & stem for full wire/housing integration including dropper post
• Change allows for more aero headtube design and reduced drag from integrated routing
• Lower, more stable road-like feel from overall geometry, chainstay length, BB drop
• Accepts 700c x 43mm tires / 650b x 51mm tires; minimum 4mm side clearance
• Trail Mixer fork dropouts can be switched for +/- 5mm selectable fork offset; 62mm optimal Trail
• Forward position=decreased trail=faster handling | Rearward position=increased trail=slower handling
• Squoval.Max tube designs, enlarged and enhanced for rough surfaces; fork/downtube integration
• Large downtube BBright bottom bracket shell w/ oversized dropped chainstays
• Carbon lay-up, round seatpost, larger volume tires contribute to rider comfort
• 1x/2x compatible, with mechanical or electronic shifting systems; removable braze-on FD mount
• Frameset
o Frame: Aspero-5 Carbon, full internal routing, Flat Mount caliper interface, 12/142mm TA dropout
o Fork: Tapered Áspero-5 Fork, Adjustable Trail; D-Shaped steerer, Flat Mount caliper interface, 12/100mm TA dropout
o Headset: FSA IS-2 1¼ x 1½"
o Thru-axles: Cervélo indexed/threaded: 12/100mm, 12/142mm
• Groupset: Shimano GRX 815 Di2 2x11 w/ hydraulic disc braking; internal eTube wiring, seatpost battery
o Dual Control levers: Di2 Shifting with mineral oil based hydraulic braking set-up
o Brake Calipers/Rotors: Shimano GRX RX815 Hydraulic Disc w/ SM-RT800 CL 160mm rotors
o Derailleurs: GRX 815 Di2 Front (braze-on), Rear (up to 34T max cog)
o Crankset/BB: GRX 810 48/31T / Cervélo BBright JY-BB 24 bottom bracket
o Cassette/Chain: HG800 11-34T / CN701
• Wheelset & Tires
o Cervélo Reserve with 32mm Carbon rims (IRW 24mm), TLR, DT350 HG11, CL disc
o Panaracer Gravel King SK TLR 700x38
• Cockpit & Accessories
o Handlebar: Carbon AB09 16° Flared Carbon, 31.8mm, integrated routing; Cervélo bar tape
o Stem: Cervélo ST32 Alloy, 31.8, (for Cervélo D-shaped fork steerer tube, integrated routing)
o Seatpost: Cervélo SP19 Carbon 27.2mm round (48-54cm Zero offset, 56-61cm, 25mm setback)
o Saddle: Prologo Dimension Nack
o Cervélo accessories: Stem Faceplate Computer/Acc mount, Rear Accessory Mount, and Smartpak 400A Top Tube Storage Bag, Downtube Protector
• Component specifics by frame size:
o 48cm Bar width: 38c-c, Stem length: 70mm, Crank length: 165mm
• Colors: Five Black
• Sizes: 48 cm
• Weight: 18.69 lbs (56cm)

Geometry
Size	48
Wheel Size	700
Stack	505mm
Reach	370mm
Head Angle	71°
Seat Angle	74.5°
Top Tube Length	512mm
Head Tube Length	83mm
Standover	690mm
Fork Trail	58.6mm
Fork Offset	52mm
Bottom Bracket Drop	78.5mm
Front Center	583mm
Wheelbase	990mm
Chainstay Length	420mm

title: 2018 Vitus Sommet
description:
The bike is easily identifiable, it is a one-of-a-kind, stripped-paint, medium 2018 Vitus Sommet bike. The blue pivot link in the middle has the original blue paint from when the bike had a blue/yellow finish. The front triangle has a noticeable dent on the bottom tube on the right side around the middle. There is no "Vitus" branding anywhere except on the black plastic bottom tube protector around the bottom bracket. The black fork has no decals because the lowers were replaced just before I had this stolen. If the frame was parted out, some suspension bearings are missing the seals.

Parts include:
Manitou Mattoc comp 160mm air fork with adjustable travel (140-160mm). Fork has red Absolute+ damper adjustment, blue rebound adjustment knob, and black air valve cap
Nukeproof Neutron 31.8mm riser bar (≈735-755mm)
Dimensions trail stem, black, 35mm length
Shorex neon neon green lock-on grips with purple ODI bar ends
Schwalbe Eddy Current front/rear tires 27.5"x2.8" with Slime sealant
RockShox Super Deluxe rear shock with piggyback. No lockout but with rebound adjustment
Shimano "Zee" BL-M640-B left brake lever for the front, Shimano Deore style rear brake lever on the right
203mm rotor on the front, 180mm rotor in the rear (both with 2-piston hydraulic calipers mounted on adapters)
15x110mm Boost Thru Axle on the front is silver, both the threaded cap and the axle itself. Boost 12x148mm boost Rear axle is black
WTB TCS i29 27.5" 650b double walled Rims both mounted tubeless, gorilla tape rim strip, different tubeless valves for each rim (one is silver, and one is a black Bontrager Round Base TLR Valve Stem, neither valve has a cap), double butted spokes
FSA tapered headset, one of the ball bearings is missing a ball
FSA HOLLOWTECH II threaded silver bottom bracket with a cracked plastic cap on one side and a direction arrow sticker being stripped
Novatec hubs loose rubber seal on the rear and damaged freehub body on the rear hub, 110mm x 15mm boost spacing on the front and 148mm x 12mm boost on the rear
Carbon fiber stem spacers (one wider than the others)
Specialized Targa Body Geometry Saddle
OneUp Components V2 dropper seat post (31.6mm diameter) and is set at the max 210mm travel with no golden spacers under the shim where there would be pill-shaped spacers
Microshift advent 11-46 9-speed black steel cassette
Microshift advent 9-speed rear derailleur, clutch version, with the plastic clutch cover taken out to expose metal internals and on/off switch
SLX - HOLLOWTECH II 2x 2-piece crankset with a bent 1x narrow-wide Shimano chainring (I believe 32t but possibly 30t) with one or several chainring threads stripped or on the verge of being stripped, non-driveside crank has plastic cap-screw
Flat black plastic pedals with adjustable metal pins, some pins missing
A 9 or 10 speed chain, probably a KMC X9 with KMC masterlink or possibly a black logoless masterlink from a Sram chain
Topeak MODULA EX ADJUSTABLE BOTTLE CAGE with white bottom piece and black top piece, yellow plastic screw on the bottom. One of the bottle cage rivet threads on the frame is slightly stripped at an angle

title: 2018 Rocky Mountain Bicycles Altitude C90
description:
Pretty much out of the box Altitude C90 model, with Shimano XT all-mountain SPD/platform pedals (M785 Trail). Pedal arms have booties at the ends to protect them from smashing the ground, Rocky-provided frame protector was never installed (instead, the bike has a long length of clear plastic adhesive on the downtube), there's a small scratch/knick on the drive side of the seat tube near the bottom bracket from when I had a spare tube zip-tied to the frame, after-market Chromag Squarewave grips. Also has a black Marsh Guard front fender with teal lettering. 
Fork	Fox 36 Float EVOL FIT4 Factory 160mm
Shock	Fox Float DPX2 Factory
Headset	FSA Orbit NO.57E
Stem	Rocky Mountain 35 CNC
Handlebar	Race Face Next R 780mm
Grips	Chromag Squarewave (black)
Brakes	Sram Guide RSC 180mm
Brake Levers	Sram Guide RSC
Shifters	Sram X01 Eagle
Front Derailleur	Rocky Mountain Spirit Guide
Rear Derailleur	Sram X01 Eagle 12 spd
Cranks & Chainrings	Sram Descendant Carbon Eagle 34T
Pedals	Shimano XT M8120 SPD pedals
Cassette	Sram XG-1295 10-50T
Chain	Sram X01 Eagle
Front Hub	Rocky Mountain Sealed Boost 15mm
Rear Hub	DT Swiss 350 Boost 148mm

title: 2014 Bianchi Volpe
description: When we introduced our first cyclocross bike to America in 1986, few riders were even into cyclocross racing. Despite that, it didn't take long for cycling enthusiasts to figure out what our new Volpe excelled at; everything! Long distance road rides. Gravel road excursions. City commuting. Loaded touring. Rail trail adventures. Neighborhood errands. The Volpe checks off all the boxes! Short of criterium racing and technical single track, the Volpe handles all types of riding efficiently and comfortably. The Volpe melds the responsive handling of a road bike with the durability of a mountain bike. A variety of surfaces and conditions can be conquered thanks to greater tire clearance and grime-shedding cantilever brakes. Our exclusive Chromolite frame and steel fork provide a smooth ride and the strength to carry whatever supplies your adventure calls for. With eyelets for front and rear racks and a set of fenders, the Volpe is one of the most versatile models we offer. Frame: Bianchi Cr-Mo, triple butted main tubes, tig welded..

title: 2011 Specialized Hardrock Sport Disc
description: My 17.5" Frame, 26" Wheels, black and white, Specialized Hardrock Sport Disc was stolen from me on July 7, 2012. according to my own research and that done by the SCPD officers investigating my case, it has been resold three times since the night it was stolen. It has traveled from Downtown Santa Cruz, to another part of Santa Cruz, to the San Jose/Bay Area where the trail runs cold. This is apparently where it now resides. The buyer was a businessman in the San Jose area, the bike was sold for $475. I miss my bike and want it back. If anyone out there finds it or has it, please be my hero and return it to me. Here are the specifics of my case, prior to it's final known sale: At 7:00 pm on 7/7/2012, I parked and locked my black and white Specialized Hardrock Sport Disc in the alleyway behind Marini's Candy Shop and the Front Street Parking Garage in downtown Santa Cruz. I wove my Kryptonite lock through the bicycle rack, both wheels, and frame of my bicycle, thinking this would deter any thief from going after my bike. I went in to one of the local shops and grabbed dinner with friends. We came out around 8:00 pm and checked to see that my bike was still there. Since the alley was full of people going back and forth on their Saturday night,  and the bike appeared to be in tact, we decided it was safe to see a movie. When we returned to get my bike around 10:10 pm, it was gone, and so was the Kryptonite lock.There are minor scratches on the right side of the frame. There was an "Another Bike Shop" sticker on the rear bottom inside of the frame when I lost it. This may no longer be on the bike. The bike had a minor shifting problem in second gear, that resulted in a bit of a hard shift when changing speeds in this gear, and was most noticeable on the uphill. There are minor cosmetic defects that ensure I can identify the bike as mine if it is found. Keeping my hopes up and fingers crossed that somebody out there comes across it and helps me out. Thank you for reading this.   Specialized A1 Premium Aluminum, fully butted, formed TT, Double ORE DT, externally relieved HT, reinforced disc mount, forged dropouts w/ replaceable hanger, disc only

title: 2009 Fuji Absolute 2.0
description: My bike, a magenta colored, fairly new, Fuji Absolute, was stolen from a bike lock-up a block North of the Burnside Bridge, parallel to the Eastern side of the Burnside Bridge Skate Park. The lock-up was near a building that looked like it was recently a business but is now abandoned and a movable chain fence. In front (to the East) there is a large empty field and behind are some bushes with a rough trail behind them leading down to a warehouse. My bike is has a grey milk cart attached to a black rack on the back of my bike with hose clamps. On the back of the milk crate there was a ripped and peeling bumper sticker that was green with white letters that read "Recycle Yourself" and had a picture of the recycling emblem. The larger part of the bumper sticker was ready to fall off and I'm not sure if it will still be on the bike or not. There was a black water bottle holder on the lower part of the bike and the chain showed slight signs of rust. Unfortunately, the reason it was stolen was because I had accidentally left my keys in the lock, which means the perpetrator probably still has the lock and key (if it is of any use I can supply you with the extra keys I have). The lock was a black lock with a thick, black, ridged, and bendable wire (for lack of a better term) that ended in a lock that the wire could insert itself into. The lock at the end was covered in black plastic that was slightly chipped at the bottom. A helmet was also with the bike. The helmet was a silver ski-style helmet, not usually used for biking, with yellow designs and various stickers on it. It had a black padded inside that was attached to a white Styrofoam piece that could be pulled out of the helmet. The keys taken with the bike had the bike on them, an old bike key and three other house keys.   Fuji Altair 2 aluminum with Tear Drop down tube, Double water bottle mounts. Rear triangle: Fuji Altair 2 custom tapered aluminum, Fuji forged road dropout with replaceable derailleur hanger

title: 2019 EEB Frame/Vector/Stealth Bomber B52, Ebike
description:
Hey all, just got my $3500 ebike stolen by a guy who said he wanted to buy it. He said he wouldn't buy it unless he tried it. God I hate myself right now. If anyone sees it posted to sell, let me know. Here's some pictures of the bike and his fake ID. 

He is a late 30's early 40's 6'3-6'5" caucasian male with light blue/green eyes. His hair is long and slicked back and has facial hair identical to the photo provided. No visible tattoos. He wore a long sleeve green jacket with tan pants.

Prior to the sheriffs arrival to my location, they were responding to a call to a nearby street for 2 men loading a bike into a u-haul attached to a red Cadillac and the callers said the whole situation seemed very suspicious. Unfortunately they did not make it in time. The sheriffs think that was my bike or the thieves were stealing multiple bikes in the area.

We met up at Yes Plaza in Rowland Heights because the plaza is well lit and the majority of the restaurants were still open during that time. The location was also in front of a Los Angeles County Sheriff service station (it turns out its only operated by volunteers and open during specific hours).

He approached me from a direction I wasn't looking at and said his girlfriend dropped him off and that she was getting gas for the truck. Initially I refused his request to demo my bike and I went around the parking lot a few times to show him that the bike is functioning. I should've stuck with my gut or at least held all his possessions as a deposit. In hindsight the thief seemed pushy and I believe he was trying to distract me from looking too closely at his ID.

I should've known better but one thing that really threw me off is when he said "I can't buy this unless I try it" I thought it was a fair point as it's a pretty expensive 2nd hand purchase. Theft wasn't on my mind as much as liability. What if he hit someone or a car? Or dropped the bike? Taking off with the bike was on the lower end of the list of my worries at the time.

There was no way for me to catch the thief as the bike reaches up to 50 mph on flat ground when the speed limiter is off.

I don't expect I'll be getting my bike back but I hope this guy gets what he deserves. 


The following are defining specs of the electric bike
EBIKE1 in reflective tape on the side of the frame
Fox 40 forks
Black 100amp controller
72v 30ah battery 
80/100 - 21 Motorcycle tubes
26" Schwalbe big apple road tires
Enduro stealth bomber frame /vector frame 
6 speed Shimano cassette w/ derailleur 
Blue under glow lightning
Scratch on right side due to loose BB that follows the curve of the pedal path

Reward for info leading to arrest or return of the bike

title: KHS Bicycles track frame
description: The bike is a bright orange KHS track frame with black v-wheels. The frame has dings and paint wear from locking up/general usage. The wheels have a distinctive double silver stripe on the side. Front wheel has silver spokes, rear spokes are black. Black seatpost, black seat, and black Intense locking grips on the handlebars. The handlebars are distinctive in that they are downhill bike-style riser bars that are cut very wide, as opposed to the popular narrow cut. The stem is a black traditional road riser stem. The headset is a silver polished aluminum Velo Orange headset. Cranks and front chainring are black Pake, fairly worn and used. The chain is black as well, but mostly from heavy usage. The drivetrain in general is greasy and worn out, and there are grease spots on the chainstay. The cranks have spots which show silver aluminum through the black paint due to wear from riding. Pedals are silver Shimano PD-M540 clipless pedals. The rear hub on the bike is one single 16 tooth fixed gear. The bike has no brakes. The bike also has black Planet Bike fenders, mounted with a special low clearance fender mounting kit (called the Reach Around kit from River City Bicycles). The fender mounting kit is silver, showing spots of rust. The fenders are very distinctive in that they are cut in half and mounted with the special kit. The tires on the front are Kenda Kontender 700C x 23 tires, the rear tire is a Continental 700C x 23.Email me, or give me a call at 541-399-4868. I will be personally high-fiving the person who returns me my bike, and we'll drink beers if that is your thing. I'm also totally willing to give someone 100 bucks for returning this thing. Unless you're the person who stole it, in which case I'd love to give you 100 karate chops in the throat.I have no proof of ownership or serial number, though my flatmates have seen the bike a million times and can vouch that it's mine.
